meta.title: Nephrology Practice Software | CKD RTM & Dialysis Coordination | clinIQ
meta.description: Complete guide to nephrology practice operations. $9,100/month in CKD RTM. CKD progression tracking. Dialysis access coordination. Transplant workup management.

hero.subhead: Your CKD patients already monitor symptoms and fluid balance — that's billable RTM. Your dialysis access planning needs coordination. Your transplant workups need tracking. clinIQ captures CKD RTM revenue and coordinates nephrology operations.

sections.content:
Nephrology manages progressive chronic disease that requires systematic tracking and timely intervention.

**The CKD monitoring opportunity:**

CKD patients should track: fluid intake, weight, blood pressure, symptoms (fatigue, swelling, appetite), medication compliance. This data informs management and identifies progression.

70 CKD patients × $130/month = $9,100/month = $109,200/year in RTM revenue.

**The progression tracking problem:**

eGFR trajectories predict outcomes. Patients progressing faster need intervention. But tracking eGFR over time, identifying acceleration, and planning for dialysis access requires organized data.

**The dialysis access timing:**

Access should be placed when eGFR reaches 15-20. Wait too long, and patients start dialysis with catheters. Place too early, and access complications occur before use.

Timing requires knowing trajectory, not just current eGFR.

**The transplant workup burden:**

Transplant workups involve dozens of tests and consultations over months. Without tracking, items fall through cracks, workups stall, and transplant listing is delayed.

CKD is ideal for RTM. Between-visit monitoring directly impacts outcomes.

**What CKD patients track:**

Fluid and weight:
- Daily weight (fluid retention indicator)
- Fluid intake
- Lower extremity edema

Blood pressure:
- Home BP readings
- Medication compliance

Symptoms:
- Fatigue and energy level
- Appetite and nausea
- Shortness of breath
- Pruritus
- Sleep quality

Medication compliance:
- Phosphate binders
- ESA (if applicable)
- Antihypertensives

**The CPT codes:**

98975: RTM setup — one-time. ~$20-25.
98977: RTM device supply. ~$50-60/month.
98980: RTM treatment management — first 20 minutes. ~$50-70/month.
98981: Additional 20-minute increments.

**What clinIQ does for CKD RTM:**

CKD-specific templates: Weight, BP, symptoms relevant to kidney disease.

Alert thresholds: Weight gain >5 lbs in week? BP consistently elevated? Worsening fatigue? Alerts trigger.

Correlation with labs: RTM symptoms contextualized with recent labs (eGFR, potassium, hemoglobin).

Pre-visit summary: RTM data available when patient arrives for appointment.

CKD management requires knowing not just where the patient is, but where they're going.

**eGFR trajectory:**

- Is eGFR stable, slowly declining, or rapidly declining?
- At current rate, when will patient reach ESRD?
- Is progression accelerating?

**What clinIQ does:**

eGFR trend visualization: All historical eGFR values plotted over time.

Projection: At current trajectory, estimated time to eGFR <15.

Acceleration detection: Rate of decline increasing → alert.

CKD stage tracking: Current stage, time at stage, stage transitions.

**Lab integration:**

CKD labs (BMP, phosphorus, PTH, hemoglobin, urinalysis) tracked with abnormal values flagged.

Anemia management: Hemoglobin trends, ESA dosing, iron studies.

Mineral bone disease: Calcium, phosphorus, PTH trends.

Timely dialysis access placement prevents catheter starts and associated complications.

**The access timeline:**

- eGFR 20-25: Begin access discussion
- eGFR 15-20: Access placement (fistula needs maturation time)
- eGFR <10-15: Dialysis initiation

Fistulas need 3-6 months to mature. Grafts need 2-4 weeks. Timing matters.

**What clinIQ does:**

Access planning timeline: Based on eGFR trajectory, recommended access planning milestones.

Surgery scheduling: Vascular surgery referral, access creation, maturation tracking.

Access maturation: Fistula/graft tracking through maturation and first use.

Catheter avoidance: Track percentage of patients starting dialysis with permanent access vs catheter.

Transplant workups are complex, multi-month processes.

**Typical workup components:**

Cardiac: Stress test, echo, possible cath
Pulmonary: CXR, PFTs, possible sleep study
GI: Colonoscopy (if indicated)
Dental: Dental clearance
Infectious: TB test, hepatitis serologies, HIV, CMV, EBV
Malignancy: Age-appropriate cancer screening
Psychosocial: Social work evaluation, financial counseling

**The tracking problem:**

25+ items across 4-6 months. Any one item falling through cracks delays listing. Patients get frustrated. Transplant centers get frustrated.

**What clinIQ does:**

Workup checklist: Every required item tracked with status (not started, ordered, scheduled, completed, reviewed).

Aging alerts: 'Cardiac stress test ordered 3 weeks ago, not scheduled.' Items stuck in the pipeline surface.

Completion visibility: What's done? What's pending? At a glance.

Transplant center coordination: Required documentation tracked. Ready for submission when complete.

**Week 1: Configuration**

CKD RTM templates, progression tracking, dialysis access timeline, transplant workup checklist configured.

**Week 2: Training and Go-Live**

Staff trained on RTM workflow, progression dashboards, workup tracking.

**ROI Timeline:**

Month 1: CKD progression visibility immediate. RTM enrollment starting.

Month 3: RTM billing at scale. Workup tracking preventing delays.

Month 6: Full RTM revenue. Access planning optimized.

**ROI Math:**

RTM revenue: 70 patients × $130/month = $9,100/month = $109,200/year

Catheter avoidance: Better access timing reduces catheter starts, improves outcomes

Workup efficiency: Faster transplant listing improves patient outcomes

**Investment:**

Professional: $499/month

Implementation: $750 one-time

faqs.q: Which CKD stages qualify for RTM?
faqs.a: Any CKD stage where between-visit monitoring informs management — typically stage 3 and above, though earlier stages with active management may qualify.

faqs.q: How do you track eGFR progression?
faqs.a: All eGFR values plotted over time. Trajectory calculated. Projection to ESRD. Acceleration detected.

faqs.q: Does this work for dialysis patients?
faqs.a: Yes. HD and PD patients can be monitored between treatments. Symptom tracking, BP, fluid management.

faqs.q: How do you coordinate transplant workups?
faqs.a: Checklist of all required items with status tracking. Aging alerts for stuck items. Completion visibility.
